mnemonics: CPU: ALU, CU, Registers Perform Ultimate Calculations
keyPoints: The ALU (Arithmetic Logic Unit) performs arithmetic and logical operations.
The Control Unit (CU) directs the operation of the processor by interpreting instructions.
Registers are small, fast storage locations inside the CPU used to hold temporary data and instructions.
commonMistakes: Confusing the Memory Address Register (MAR) with the Memory Data Register (MDR). MAR holds the address to read from/write to, MDR holds the actual data.
Thinking the ALU has its own storage; it operates on data fetched from registers and sends the result back to a register.
